story: Floods of mice overrun my house, chanting for cheese. They warn that mice are fond of meat and press me to keep cutting triangles. At last the fridge is bare. “Mouse! There is more cheese!” I say. “Not here—at the store. I’ll fetch my wallet, drive down, return with mountains.” As I say it, I decide that if they let me step outside, I’ll bolt—across town, across the sea—and never return. The head mouse peers at my empty shelves; the crowd rumbles and licks their lips.
implausible_knowledge: As I say it, I decide that if they let me step outside, I’ll bolt—across town, across the sea—and never return.
question: When the narrator offers to go buy more cheese and the kitchen falls silent, what will the head mouse do?
answer_with_knowledge: Send several mice to accompany him and keep him from escaping.
answer_without_knowledge: Allow the narrator to leave alone to get more cheese.
